raw_response_text:
<features>
- observable feature: presence of parallel wooden planks
- observable feature: presence of a hand holding a tool
- observable feature: visible wood grain and texture
</features>
<kb>
- IF [observable visual features = parallel wooden planks AND observable visual features = hand holding tool] THEN [17]
- IF [observable visual features = pothole AND observable visual features = water] THEN [32]
</kb>
<rule_check>
- Rule 1 fires because the image shows parallel wooden planks and a hand holding a tool, which matches the condition for class 17.
</rule_check>
<response>17</response>

raw_response_text:
<features>
- observable feature: presence of flowing water
- observable feature: water flowing through narrow rocky channels
- observable feature: rocks surrounding the water
</features>
<kb>
- IF [presence of flowing water AND water flowing through narrow rocky channels AND rocks surrounding the water] THEN [17]
- IF [presence of puddles or holes in ground] THEN [32]
</kb>

<rule_check>
- Rule 1 fires because the Query Image shows flowing water in narrow rocky channels surrounded by rocks, which matches the visual pattern defined for class 17.
</rule_check>

<response>17</response>

Then formulate a knowledge base (kb) with a small set of IF-THEN rules grounded in observable patterns from the labeled examples in the support images and the Query Image, for all classes. Try to unify the conditions so that there is only one rule per class.
Rules must refer only to visible features.
Then check which rule(s) are best satisfied by the Query Image.

- observable feature: feature value
- observable feature: feature value
</features>
<kb>
- IF [observable visual features = feature value AND/OR observable visual features = feature value] THEN [class1]
- IF [observable visual features = feature value] THEN [class2]
</kb>

<rule_check>

referring only to the features listed above. Do not discuss other rules or introduce new features.
</rule_check>

message.content:
<features>
- observable feature: presence of flowing water
- observable feature: water flowing through narrow rocky channels
- observable feature: rocks surrounding the water
</features>
<kb>
- IF [presence of flowing water AND water flowing through narrow rocky channels AND rocks surrounding the water] THEN [17]
- IF [presence of puddles or holes in ground] THEN [32]
</kb>

<rule_check>

</rule_check>
